_title: BS 7671:2018+A2:2022 Regulation 522 — IP Rating Selection by Environment
_basis: BS EN 60529 — Degrees of protection provided by enclosures (IP Code). BS 7671 Section 522 references this for protection against external influences.
_note: IP ratings are TWO digits: first = solids/dust, second = liquids/water. A third digit (impact, IK code) is sometimes added but is a separate standard.

first_digit_solids_dust.1: Protected against solid objects ≥ 50mm (e.g. back of hand)
first_digit_solids_dust.3: Protected against solid objects ≥ 2.5mm (e.g. tools, thick wires)
first_digit_solids_dust.4: Protected against solid objects ≥ 1mm (e.g. most wires, screws)
first_digit_solids_dust.5: Dust-protected — dust may enter but not in quantity to interfere with operation

second_digit_water.2: Protected against dripping water when tilted up to 15°
second_digit_water.3: Protected against spraying water (60° from vertical)
second_digit_water.4: Protected against splashing water from any direction
second_digit_water.6: Protected against powerful water jets (e.g. shipboard washdown)
second_digit_water.7: Protected against temporary immersion (15cm to 1m depth, 30 min)
second_digit_water.9: Protected against high-temperature, high-pressure water jets (food industry)

common_ip_ratings_explained.IP20: Indoor only. No protection against water. Finger-safe (no exposed live parts). Standard for indoor accessories in dry rooms.
common_ip_ratings_explained.IP21: Indoor with dripping water protection. Boiler rooms, plant rooms.
common_ip_ratings_explained.IP44: Outdoor general purpose. Splash-proof. External lighting, garden sockets.
common_ip_ratings_explained.IP55: Dust-protected with jet water resistance. Industrial outdoor, car washes.
common_ip_ratings_explained.IP65: Dust-tight with jet water resistance. Demanding outdoor, exposed installations.
common_ip_ratings_explained.IP66: Dust-tight with powerful jet resistance. Marine, heavy industrial.
common_ip_ratings_explained.IP67: Dust-tight with temporary immersion resistance. Flooding-prone areas.
common_ip_ratings_explained.IP68: Dust-tight with continuous immersion. Submersible equipment (pumps, pool lights).

minimum_ip_by_location._reference: BS 7671:2018 Reg 522.3 and Part 7 special locations

habitable_rooms_dry.rationale: Finger-safe only — no water protection needed.

bathroom_zone_1_above_bath_shower_to_2.2._note: IPx5 if water jets used (e.g. body jets)

bathroom_outside_zones.minimum: IP21 (general) or IPx4 if window or door opens onto wet area

garage_workshop_domestic._note: Higher if power washing or solvents in use

external_exposed_to_weather.minimum: IP55 to IP66 depending on exposure

computer_server_rooms._note: Sealed cabinets to IP54 to prevent dust

warehouse_indoor.minimum: IP21 to IP44 depending on forklift/cleaning regime

food_production.minimum: IP66 or IP69K — daily high-pressure cleaning

chemical_processing.minimum: IP66 + corrosion-resistant materials per BS EN 14122

imaging_room.minimum: IP21 (general) with EMC shielding for MRI suite

science_laboratory.minimum: IP44 above bench, IP20 at ceiling

animal_houses._note: Ammonia and moisture from livestock — corrosion-resistant

ev_charging_post_outdoor.minimum: IP54 (general), IP55 with operating cable connected, IP44 with operating cable disconnected — per BS EN 61851-1

impact_protection_ik_code._reference: BS EN 62262 — Degrees of protection provided by enclosures for electrical equipment against external mechanical impacts (IK code)
impact_protection_ik_code._note: IK is a separate standard from IP. Specified alongside IP where mechanical risk is significant.

selection_principle.rule: Select the IP rating from the WORST credible environmental condition the equipment will face during its lifetime — including cleaning, flooding, and abnormal events.
common_under_specification: External equipment specified IP44 then exposed to power washing (needs IP65 minimum)
Kitchen worktop sockets at IP20 (need IP44 — cleaning splashes)
Pump pit equipment at IP54 (needs IP67 — periodic flooding)
Coastal exposed equipment at IP54 (needs IP66 — salt spray + driving rain)

ip_does_not_mean.no_corrosion_protection: IP66 plastic enclosure may still corrode internal components in salty/chemical atmospheres — specify materials separately
ip_does_not_mean.no_temperature_rating: IP rating says nothing about operating temperature — check separately
ip_does_not_mean.no_uv_resistance: Plastic enclosures may degrade in sunlight — specify UV-stable materials for external
ip_does_not_mean.no_emc_shielding: IP is about physical ingress only — EMC requires conductive enclosure with proper bonding
